name: pympdata-examples

metadata.name: PyMPDATA-examples
metadata.summary: PyMPDATA usage examples reproducing results from literature and depicting how to use PyMPDATA in Python from Jupyter notebooks

documentation:
# Introduction PyMPDATA examples are bundled with PyMPDATA and located in the examples subfolder. They constitute a separate PyMPDATA_examples Python package which is also available at PyPI. The examples have additional dependencies listed in PyMPDATA_examples package setup.py file. Running the examples requires the PyMPDATA_examples package to be installed. We recommend you look through the example gallery below to see the examples in action. # Example gallery Unless stated otherwise the following examples solve the basic advection equation: $$ \partial_t (\psi) + \nabla \cdot (u \psi) = 0 $$ The examples are grouped by the dimensionality of the computational grid. ## in 1D | tags | link | |:--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|:---------------------------------------------------------| | advection-diffusion equation
$$ \partial_t (\psi) + \nabla \cdot (u \psi) + \mu \Delta (\psi) = 0 $$ | advection_diffusion_1d* | | Black-Scholes equation, option pricing
$$ \frac{\partial f}{\partial t} + rS \frac{\partial f}{\partial S} + \frac{\sigma^2}{2} S^2 \frac{\partial^2 f}{\partial S^2} - rf = 0$$ | Arabas_and_Farhat_2020* | | advection equation, homogeneous, several algorithm variants comparison: infinite-gauge, flux-corrected,.. | Smolarkiewicz_2006_Figs_3_4_10_11_12 | | Size-spectral advection, particle population condensational growth, coordinate transformation
$$ \partial_t (G \psi) + \nabla \cdot (Gu \psi) = 0 $$ | Olesik_et_al_2022* | | advection equation, [double-pass donor-cell option](https://osti.gov/servlets/purl/7049237) | DPDC | ## in 2D | tags | link | |:--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|:-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | advection-diffusion equation
$$ \partial_t (\psi) + \nabla \cdot (u \psi) + \mu \Delta (\psi) = 0 $$ | advection_diffusion_2d*
| | Spectral-spatial advection, particle population condensational growth in a vertical column of air, time dependent flow | Shipway_and_Hill_2012
| | shallow-water equations
$$\begin{eqnarray} \partial_t h + \partial_x (uh) + \partial_y (vh) &=& 0~ \\\ \partial_t (uh) + \partial_x ( uuh) + \partial_y (vuh) &=& - h \partial_x h~ \\\ \partial_t (vh) + \partial_x ( uvh) + \partial_y (vvh) &=& - h \partial_y h~ \end{eqnarray}$$ | Jarecka_et_al_2015* | | advection equation, solid body rotation | Molenkamp_test_as_in_Jaruga_et_al_2015_Fig_12* | | advection equation, coordinate transformation, spherical coordinates, see also examples in [PyMPDATA-MPI](https://pypi.org/project/PyMPDATA-MPI/) $$ \partial_t (G \psi) + \nabla \cdot (Gu \psi) = 0 $$ | Williamson_and_Rasch_1989_as_in_Jaruga_et_al_2015_Fig_14
| | advection equation, comparison against DG solution using [Trixi.jl](https://trixi-framework.github.io/) | trixi_comparison | ## in 3D | tags | link | |:------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|:---------------------------------------| | homogeneous advection equation | Smolarkiewicz_1984 | | homogeneous advection equation, performance comparison against libmpdata++, scalability analysis in respect to threads | Bartman_et_al_2022 | \* - with comparison against analytic solution # Installation Since the examples package includes Jupyter notebooks (and their execution requires write access), the suggested install and launch steps are: git clone https://github.com/open-atmos/PyMPDATA-examples.git cd PyMPDATA-examples pip install -e . jupyter-notebook Alternatively, one can also install the examples package from pypi.org by using pip install PyMPDATA-examples.

modules.name: PyMPDATA_examples.DPDC
documentation:
This example demonstrates the use of the Double-Pass Donor-Cell option in `PyMPDATA.options`.

demo.ipynb:
.. include:: ./demo.ipynb.badges.md

modules.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Smolarkiewicz_1984
documentation:
This is an example of 3D advection, a spherical signal revolving in a box, based on
\[Smolarkiewicz, 1984\](<https://doi.org/10.1016/0021-9991(84)90121-9>).

figs_13-14.ipynb:
.. include:: ./figs_13-14.ipynb.badges.md

methods.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Smolarkiewicz_1984.settings.Settings.advector
documentation: constant angular velocity rotational field

classes.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Shipway_and_Hill_2012.droplet_activation.DropletActivation
documentation: pylint: disable=too-few-public-methods

modules.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Shipway_and_Hill_2012
documentation:
This is an example of 2D droplet size-spectral/spatial problem of
condensational growth in a column of air,
as described in \[Shipway and Hill 2012\](<https://doi.org/10.1002/qj.1913>).

fig_1.ipynb
.. include:: ./fig_1.ipynb.badges.md

methods.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Shipway_and_Hill_2012.mpdata.MPDATA.__init__
documentation: pylint: disable=too-few-public-methods

documentation: A14 in libcloudph++ 1.0 paper

functions.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Shipway_and_Hill_2012.formulae.pressure
documentation: A15 in libcloudph++ 1.0 paper

modules.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Smolarkiewicz_2006_Figs_3_4_10_11_12
documentation:
This is an example of 1D homogeneous advection test from
\[Smolarkiewicz 2006\](<https://doi.org/10.1002/fld.1071>), depicting infinite-gauge and
flux-corrected transport case.

demo.ipynb:
.. include:: ./demo.ipynb.badges.md

!\[plot\](<https://github.com/open-atmos/PyMPDATA/releases/download/tip/fig_4.svg>)

modules.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Molenkamp_test_as_in_Jaruga_et_al_2015_Fig_12
documentation:
This is an example based on the solid-body rotation test from
\[Molenkap 1968\](<http://doi.org/10.1175/1520-0450(1968)007%3C0160:AOFDMA%3E2.0.CO;2>)
(as in Fig. 12 in \[Jaruga et al. 2015\](<https://doi.org/10.5194/gmd-8-1005-2015>)).

demo.ipynb:
.. include:: ./demo.ipynb.badges.md

modules.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Arabas_and_Farhat_2020
documentation:
This example implements simulations presented in the

: \[Arabas and Farhat 2020\](<https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cam.2019.05.023>)
  study on pricing of European and American options using MPDATA.

Each notebook in this directory corresponds to a figure or a table in the paper.

.. include:: ./fig_1.ipynb.badges.md

modules.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Jarecka_et_al_2015
documentation:
This module showcases the PyMPDATA implementation of an MPDATA-based shallow-water equations
solver discussed and bencharked against analytical solutions in
\[Jarecka_et_al_2015\](<https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jcp.2015.02.003>).

fig_6.ipynb:
.. include:: ./fig_6.ipynb.badges.md

modules.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Jarecka_et_al_2015.formulae
documentation: adapted from:
https://github.com/igfuw/shallow-water-elliptic-drop/blob/master/analytical/analytic_equations.py
Code used in the paper of Jarecka, Jaruga, Smolarkiewicz -
"A Spreading Drop of Shallow Water" (JCP 289, doi:10.1016/j.jcp.2015.02.003).

functions.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Jarecka_et_al_2015.formulae.deriv
documentation:
return derivatives of [lambda_x, dlambda_x/dt, lambda_y, dlambda_y/dt
four first-order ODEs based on  eq. 7  (Jarecka, Jaruga, Smolarkiewicz)

modules.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Olesik_et_al_2022

This is a PyMPDATA implementation of 1D particle population condensational growth
problem with coordinate transformations, as presented in
\[Olesik et al. 2022\](<https://doi.org/10.5194/gmd-15-3879-2022>).

modules.name: PyMPDATA_examples.Williamson_and_Rasch_1989_as_in_Jaruga_et_al_2015_Fig_14
documentation:
This example is based on
\[Williamson and Rasch 1989\](<https://doi.org/10.1175/1520-0493(1989)117%3C0102:TDSLTW%3E2.0.CO;2>).
It demonstrates the use of `PyMPDATA.solver.g_factor`
to transform the advection problem onto a sphere.
